definition: Supply-normalization disinflation (2022-24 type) compresses credit spreads while demand-destruction disinflation widens them — a falsifiable and directly tradeable distinction that markets are currently not discriminating between.
evidence: Supply chain normalization and inventory restocking drove 2022-24 goods disinflation with spread compression
Demand-destruction disinflation (recessionary) historically widens credit spreads
Current market pricing does not discriminate between these mechanisms
open_questions: How quickly will markets reprice if the next phase of disinflation is demand-destruction rather than supply-normalization?
